key_factors: Netherlands is ranked 8th in the world (as of June 2026), while Sweden is ranked 34th.
Both teams played their openers on June 14, 2026, giving each side 5 full rest days heading into this match.
The match is held at Houston's NRG Stadium, where the retractable roof is confirmed to be closed and climate-controlled, neutralizing any potential weather impact.
Both teams enter with positive momentum: Sweden won their opener 5-1 against Tunisia, while the Netherlands drew 2-2 with Japan.
In the last 10 years, the teams have met twice in competitive matches (2018 World Cup qualifiers), with the Netherlands winning 2-0 at home and drawing 1-1 away.
sources: https://www.fifa.com (FIFA World Rankings and Official Match Schedule)

key_factors: Germany is ranked 9th in the world, while Côte d'Ivoire is ranked 33rd.
Côte d'Ivoire starting center back Evan Ndicka is officially ruled out due to a hamstring/thigh injury, forcing a rotation in the Ivorian defense.
Both teams enter with 3 points: Germany won their opener 7-1 against Curaçao, while Côte d'Ivoire defeated Ecuador 1-0.
Germany traveled ~1,200 miles from Houston to Toronto, whereas Côte d'Ivoire traveled only ~350 miles from Philadelphia.
The teams have never played a competitive match against each other in their senior history, with their only prior meeting being a 2-2 friendly draw in 2009.

key_factors: Ecuador is ranked 23rd in the world, while Curaçao is ranked 82nd.

Ecuador and Curaçao have never faced each other in an official competitive match in their history.
Both teams have had 5 full days of rest since their opening games on June 14.
